title: ferray-numpy-interop: to_arrow always copies — claims zero-copy but never achieves it
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

The doc claims 'Zero-copy when the ferray array is C-contiguous' but to_arrow() in arrow_conv.rs always copies: 'let data: Vec<T> = match self.as_slice() { Some(slice) => slice.to_vec(), None => self.to_vec_flat() }'. Even when as_slice() succeeds (C-contiguous), it calls .to_vec() which allocates and copies. Arrow's Buffer::from_vec then takes ownership of that Vec. True zero-copy would use unsafe pointer sharing or Arrow's Buffer::from_custom_allocation to point at the ferray array's memory. The comment 'SAFETY: buffer length is exactly self.size() * size_of::<T>()' is misleading — there's no unsafe here, just a copy.
